title: Quick 20‑Minute Breakfast: Egg & Chorizo Skewers for Early Risers
keyword: breakfast egg chorizo skewers gyro pan
meta_description: Whip up a protein‑packed breakfast in just 20 minutes with egg & chorizo skewers. Learn how to use a Vertical Gyro Pan with Skewer for Oven for perfect results
html:
Looking for a speedy, satisfying breakfast that feels a little gourmet? Try these breakfast egg chorizo skewers cooked in a gyro pan. With just a handful of ingredients and a Vertical Gyro Pan with Skewer for Oven, you can have a hot, handheld meal ready in under 20 minutes—ideal for early risers who still want flavor.

Why Skewers Make a Better Breakfast

Skewering the ingredients does more than look cool. It maximizes surface area, so the eggs set quickly while the chorizo releases its smoky oils onto every bite. The vertical design of the gyro pan creates a gentle, even heat that mimics a rotisserie, preventing the edges from over‑cooking while the center stays tender.

Gather Your Ingredients (Ready in 5 Minutes)

  • 4 large eggs
  • 6 ounces Mexican chorizo, crumbled
  • 1 small red bell pepper, cut into 1‑inch squares
  • 1 small onion, cut into wedges
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • Salt, pepper, and a pinch of smoked paprika
  • Fresh cilantro or parsley for garnish

All of these items can be pre‑pped while the pan heats, so you stay within the 20‑minute window.

Step‑By‑Step: Cooking the Egg & Chorizo Skewers

1. Preheat the Vertical Gyro Pan with Skewer for Oven to 425°F (220°C). The stainless‑steel body heats evenly and holds temperature well, which is crucial for a quick bake.

2. While the pan warms, whisk the eggs in a bowl with a pinch of salt, pepper, and smoked paprika. Set aside.

3. In a skillet, cook the crumbled chorizo over medium heat for 2–3 minutes until it starts to brown. Drain excess fat if desired, then let it cool slightly.

4. Assemble the skewers: thread a piece of bell pepper, a wedge of onion, a spoonful of chorizo, and finally a raw egg (you can crack the egg onto the skewer and let it spread). Repeat until the skewers are full.

5. Brush the assembled skewers lightly with olive oil and place them vertically in the gyro pan. The vertical orientation allows the heat to circulate around each skewer, cooking the egg from all sides.

6. Roast for 8‑10 minutes, checking at the 8‑minute mark. The egg should be set but still slightly custardy, and the chorizo should be crisp on the edges.

7. Remove the skewers, sprinkle with fresh cilantro, and serve immediately. They pair well with a side of avocado slices or a simple salsa.

Tips for Perfect Skewers Every Time

• Keep the eggs cold until the last moment. Cold eggs hit the hot pan and set faster, preventing a rubbery texture.

• Don’t overload the skewer. Too many ingredients can cause the egg to slide off. Aim for a balanced ratio: one egg, a pinch of chorizo, and a bite‑size piece of vegetable.

• Use the gyro pan’s built‑in handle. The stainless steel vertical skewer pan is designed for easy insertion and removal, so you can flip the skewers halfway through cooking for an even golden color.

• Experiment with flavors. Swap out bell pepper for cherry tomatoes, or add a slice of cheese on top of the egg for a melty finish.

Cleaning and Storing Your Gyro Pan

After the breakfast rush, let the Vertical Gyro Pan with Skewer for Oven cool completely. Because it’s stainless steel, a simple soak in warm, soapy water will release any stuck bits. Use a non‑abrasive sponge to avoid scratching the surface. Once clean, dry thoroughly and store in a dry cabinet; the pan’s sturdy construction means it will last for years of weekday breakfasts.

Nutrition Snapshot

Each skewer delivers roughly 180 calories, 12 g of protein, and a satisfying dose of healthy fats from the egg yolk and olive oil. The chorizo adds a spicy kick without overwhelming the dish, making it a balanced start to your day.

FAQ

Can I use a regular oven rack instead of the gyro pan?

You can, but the vertical design of the gyro pan ensures even heat distribution and quicker cooking. A flat rack may cause the eggs to sit unevenly, leading to over‑cooked edges.

What other proteins work well on these skewers?

Besides chorizo, try diced turkey sausage, cooked shrimp, or even crumbled tofu for a vegetarian twist. The key is to pre‑cook any raw meat before threading it onto the skewer.

Is the gyro pan safe for broiling?

Yes. The stainless‑steel construction is broiler‑safe, and the vertical orientation helps keep the food away from direct flame, reducing the risk of burning.
